title: notifications core — error codes
description: Module-scoped error codes. Every code follows foundation's envelope contract: uppercase snake_case, module-namespaced prefix `NOTIFICATIONS_`. Downstream error codes exist per channel module (NOTIFICATIONS_MAIL_HARD_BOUNCE, NOTIFICATIONS_SMS_PROVIDER_ERROR, NOTIFICATIONS_PUSH_INVALID_TOKEN).

errors.description: Template resolver could not locate a template for (category, channel, locale). Fallback template used + this event logged. Indicates missing seed OR missing render artifact.
Template body failed Blade validation on publish OR referenced a missing variable at send. Publish is refused; send falls back to platform default + logs.
Caller dispatched a category slug that isn't in the registry. Almost always a code bug (typo, missing notifications.json entry). Dispatch refused.
Channel module reported disabled via its kill-switch OR feature flag. Dispatch skips the channel but continues with other channels. Not an error to the caller — event logged internally.
Tenant reached its `notifications.<channel>.month` cap. Dispatch refused for that channel. Tenant admin sees upsell path in the response.
Recipient has opt_out_allowed=true category with enabled=false. NOT surfaced as an error to callers — dispatch returns success with the delivery marked suppressed. Logged for audit trail.
ProcessDigestJob raced with an idempotent duplicate — the second run detected a delivered digest for the same window. Job noops.
Caller passed a payload that fails the category's declared variable schema. Dispatch refused. Indicates a code bug in the caller.
Dispatch call provided no recipient AND the caller couldn't be inferred (e.g. dispatched from a queue job with no auth context). Dispatch refused.
Recipient's consent_age_gate blocks the category (e.g. marketing to minor_under_consent_age). Dispatch refused with clear reason.
Tenant is in suspended state — dispatch queued but not delivered. Retryable when tenant unsuspends.
Signed unsubscribe token failed verification OR expired. Note: user's existing preference stays intact — they weren't RE-subscribed by a bad link.
Attempted to set enabled=false on a category with opt_out_allowed=false (transactional_required). Refused. Include category display_name + explanation in message.
Attempted to delete a NotificationTemplate that has Notification rows in a non-terminal state referencing it. Delete refused.
notifications.dispatch kill switch is off. Dispatch suppressed. Retry-After header set based on incident-response ETA.
